__description: The wifi module manages WIFI connections

name.__description: The wifi.name property gets the current connected wireless network SSID, or nil if the computer is not connected.
name.__example:
--! luart-extensions
import wifi

local ssid = wifi.name
if not ssid then
  print("You are not connected to a wireless network") 
else
  print("You are connected to the wireless network '"..ssid.."'")
end

connected.__description: The wifi.connected property checks if the computer is connected to a wireless network, returning a boolean value.
connected.__example:
--! luart-extensions
import wifi

if wifi.connected then
  print("You are connected to the '"..wifi.name.."' wireless network") 
else
  print("You are not connected to a wireless network")
end
connected.__tinydesc: Checks if the computer is connected to a wireless network

mac.__description: The wifi.mac property gets the wireless access point MAC address as a string or nil if not connected.
mac.__example:
--! luart-extensions
import wifi

if wifi.connected then
  print("WIFI endpoint MAC adress : "..wifi.mac) 
else
  print("You are not connected to a wireless network")
end
mac.__tinydesc: Gets the wireless access point mac adress

quality.__description: The wifi.quality property gets the current wireless connection signal quality as a number, in percent.
quality.__example:
--! luart-extensions
import wifi

if wifi.connected then
  print("WIFI signal quality is "..wifi.quality.."%") 
else
  print("You are not connected to a wireless network")
end
quality.__tinydesc: Gets the WIFI signal quality of the current connection

channel.__description: The wifi.channel property gets the current wireless connection channel.
channel.__example:
--! luart-extensions
import wifi

if wifi.connected then
  print("WIFI channel is "..wifi.channel) 
else
  print("You are not connected to a wireless network")
end

connect.__description:
The wifi.connect() function try to connect to the specified WIFI endpoint. If the endpoint is already known by Windows, password won't be needed.

Notes

  • The function returns immediately. A true result just means the connection process has successfully started.
  • To check for a successful connection you can use the wifi.onConnected event.

__params.__description: A string representing the password if the wireless network is not already known by Windows.

An optional string representing the authentification method to use among those values :

  • "WPA" : PA-Enterprise 802.11 authentication.
  • "WPAPSK" : WPA-Personal 802.11 authentication.
  • "WPA2" : WPA2-Enterprise 802.11 authentication.
  • "WPA2PSK" : WPA2-Personal 802.11 authentication (the default).
  • "WPA3ENT192" : WPA3-Enterprise 192-bit mode authentication.

An optional string representing the encryption method to use among those values :

  • "none" : no encryption
  • "WEP" – WEP encryption
  • "TKIP" : TKIP encryption
  • "AES" : AES encryption (the default)
  • "GCMP256" : GCMP256 encryption

connect.__returns: Returns a boolean value indicating if the connection process has correctly started.
connect.__example:
--! luart-extensions
import wifi

function wifi.onConnected()
  print("You are connected to "..wifi.name)
  sys.exit()
end

wifi.connect("my wifi endpoint")

waitall()

disconnect.__description:
The wifi.disconnect() function tries to disconnect from the WIFI endpoint.

Notes

  • The function returns immediately. A true result just means the disconnection process has successfully started.
  • To check for a successful disconnection you can use the wifi.onDisconnected event.
disconnect.__returns: Returns a boolean value indicating if the disconnection process has correctly started.
disconnect.__example:
--! luart-extensions
import wifi

function wifi.onDisconnected()
  print("You are disconnected from the wireless network !")
  sys.exit()
end

wifi.connect("your wifi endpoint")

-- sleeps for 5sec then disconnect
sleep(5000)

wifi.disconnect()

waitall()

scan.__description:
The wifi.scan() function scans for available networks. Once completed, the found endpoints are available in the returned table.

Notes

  • The function returns immediately after starting the scan.
  • The scan process may take a few seconds to complete.
scan.__returns:
Returns the list of found endpoints, as a table. Each endpoint is a table with the following fields:
  • name: WIFI endpoint SSID
  • quality: signal quality in percent
  • secured: endpoint uses secured authentication
  • known: endpoint is already known by Windows (no authentication needed)
scan.__example:
print("Scanning WIFI endpoints...")

for endpoint in each(wifi.scan()) do
  print("----- Wifi endpoint ".. endpoint.name)
  print("signal :", endpoint.signal)
  print("secured :", endpoint.secured)
  print("known : ", endpoint.known)
end

onConnected.__description: The wifi.onConnected() event is thrown each time the computer has successfully connected to a WIFI endpoint.
onConnected.__tinydesc: Event fired when computer has successfully connected to WIFI endpoint
onConnected.__example:
--! luart-extensions
import wifi
local ui = require "ui"

function wifi.onConnected()
  ui.info("You are now connected to "..wifi.name.." WIFI endpoint")
end

function wifi.onDisconnected()
  ui.warn("You are now disconnected from WIFI endpoint")
end

-- wait 10 minutes (600,000 milliseconds), then exit
sleep(600000)

onDisconnected.__description: The wifi.onDisconnected() event is thrown each time the computer has lost its connection to WIFI endpoint.
onDisconnected.__tinydesc: Event fired when computer has lost its connection to WIFI endpoint
onDisconnected.__example:
--! luart-extensions
import wifi
local ui = require "ui"

function wifi.onConnected()
  ui.info("You are now connected to "..wifi.name.." WIFI endpoint")
end

function wifi.onDisconnected()
  ui.warn("You are now disconnected from WIFI endpoint")
end

-- wait 10 minutes (600,000 milliseconds), then exit
sleep(600000)
